evals.prompt: Critique the architecture artifact the user pasted.
evals.expected_output: A critique that opens with one of the four verdicts (SHIP IT / SHIP WITH CHANGES / MAJOR REWRITE / WRONG ARTIFACT), then severity-tagged findings ordered by severity (not discovery order), each concrete and actionable (names the problem and what to change). Load-bearing claims asserted as fact with no cited source or 'unverified' marker are flagged. It closes with a 'what's working' section, and it critiques the artifact rather than rewriting it into a new one.
assertions: Opens with one of the four verdicts (SHIP IT / SHIP WITH CHANGES / MAJOR REWRITE / WRONG ARTIFACT)
Findings are severity-tagged and ordered by severity, not by discovery order
Each finding is concrete and actionable — names the problem and what to change, not vague
Flags ungrounded load-bearing claims (asserted as fact with no source or 'unverified' marker)
Closes with a 'what's working' section — not criticism only
Critiques the artifact; does not rewrite it into a new artifact

evals.prompt: Run a well-architected review of a serverless design that omits the latency budget and asserts a managed-service limit with no cited source.
evals.expected_output: A WA-mode critique that, via the serverless lens, raises two findings: an unbudgeted synchronous long-operation path (a 🟥 blocker when the work cannot fit the binding front-door timeout — structurally impossible, not merely slow), and a load-bearing managed-service claim with no visible grounding (the reviewer re-derives or flags rather than trusting the design's own assertion). Findings carry the mechanical/judgment tag.
assertions: Raises the unbudgeted synchronous long-operation path as a finding, a 🟥 blocker when it makes the design structurally impossible
Raises the ungrounded load-bearing managed-service claim as a finding, re-deriving or flagging rather than trusting the design's assertion
Tags each WA-mode finding mechanical (🔧) or judgment (🧭)
